Home News

#StopRansomware: Play Ransomware

Note: This joint Cybersecurity Advisory is part of an ongoing #StopRansomware effort. Originally published December 18, 2023.

The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA), and Australian Signals Directorate's Australian Cyber Security Centre (ASD's ACSC) are releasing this joint Cybersecurity Advisory (CSA) to disseminate known Play ransomware IOCs and TTPs.

Technical Details

Play ransomware actors employ a double-extortion model, encrypting systems after exfiltrating data. Ransom notes do not include an initial ransom demand or payment instructions.

The actors gain initial access via valid accounts (T1078) and exploitation of public-facing applications.

Table 3: Play Ransomware Actors ATT&CK Techniques for Enterprise - Reconnaissance

Technique TitleIDUse
Active Scanning T1595 Play actors scan victim networks for vulnerabilities prior to compromise.
Gather Victim Network Information T1590 Play actors collect network topology information.

Table 4: ATT&CK Techniques for Enterprise - Initial Access

Technique TitleIDUse
Exploit Public-Facing Application T1190 Play actors exploit known vulnerabilities in FortiOS and Microsoft Exchange.
External Remote Services T1133 Play actors leverage compromised VPN credentials for initial access.
Valid Accounts T1078 Domain accounts used for initial access and lateral movement.

Table 5: ATT&CK Techniques for Enterprise - Execution

Technique TitleIDUse
Command and Scripting Interpreter: PowerShell T1059.001 Play actors execute PowerShell scripts for discovery.
Command and Scripting Interpreter: Windows Command Shell T1059.003 Play actors use cmd.exe for command execution.

Table 11: ATT&CK Techniques for Enterprise - Impact

Technique TitleIDUse
Data Encrypted for Impact T1486 Play ransomware encrypts files using AES-RSA hybrid encryption.

Mitigations

FBI, CISA, and ASD's ACSC recommend testing your existing security controls inventory to assess how they perform against the ATT&CK techniques described in this advisory.
